explanations.rationale: Action 2 has highest posterior belief Va=1.200 with margin 1.300 over next-best alternative. | Direct pathway Go signal for action 2 dominates indirect No-Go (ratio=4.62). Thalamic disinhibition pathway is active. | Dopamine dominant — exploitation mode active. System favours high-value known actions.
explanations.pathway_rationale: Direct pathway Go signal for action 2 dominates indirect No-Go (ratio=4.62). Thalamic disinhibition pathway is active.
explanations.counterfactual_comparison:
If wait were chosen: reward gap = +0.950, confidence = 1.00. wait was rejected: wait had lower risk-adjusted utility Q_risk=-0.150 vs Q_risk=0.800 for press_button (gap=+0.950, rho=0.50); Bayesian belief lower for wait: Va=-0.400 vs Va=1.200; GPi gate margin for wait=-0.4500 vs press_button=0.2500 — direct pathway insufficient; DA-dominant exploitation mode (DA=0.65) favours known high-value action.
  If reach_right were chosen: reward gap = +0.550, confidence = 0.83. reach_right was rejected: reach_right had lower risk-adjusted utility Q_risk=0.250 vs Q_risk=0.800 for press_button (gap=+0.550, rho=0.50); Bayesian belief lower for reach_right: Va=-0.100 vs Va=1.200; GPi gate margin for reach_right=-0.2000 vs press_button=0.2500 — direct pathway insufficient; DA-dominant exploitation mode (DA=0.65) favours known high-value action.
  If reach_left were chosen: reward gap = +0.500, confidence = 0.88. reach_left was rejected: reach_left had lower risk-adjusted utility Q_risk=0.300 vs Q_risk=0.800 for press_button (gap=+0.500, rho=0.50); Bayesian belief lower for reach_left: Va=-0.300 vs Va=1.200; GPi gate margin for reach_left=-0.3000 vs press_button=0.2500 — direct pathway insufficient; DA-dominant exploitation mode (DA=0.65) favours known high-value action.
explanations.neuromodulatory_summary: Neuromodulator state: DA=0.650, 5HT=0.350, NE=0.300. Mt=0.520. Dominant=DA (exploitation mode). alpha_t=0.06300, rho=0.500.
